description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer who has been handed a plain-text log file that records the health of a single production API.  
    The log file already exists on disk at:

        /home/user/logs/service_status.log

    Each line in that file follows the exact pattern:

        YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ <STATUS> [latency]

    • <STATUS> is either the word UP or DOWN  
    • If the status is UP a millisecond latency value (for example “48ms”) is present after a single space.  
    • If the status is DOWN the line ends immediately after the word DOWN (there is no trailing space or latency value).  

    Your task:  
    1. Read the log file.  
    2. Print to STDOUT only the timestamps for every line whose status is DOWN.  
       • Output the timestamps in the same order they appear in the file.  
       • Output each timestamp on its own line.  
       • Output nothing else: no extra spaces, headers, blank lines, counts, or status words.  
       • Do not write anything to any other file; the required result should appear only in the terminal.  

    IMPORTANT: The automated test will pass your session’s captured STDOUT through `diff` against an oracle file that contains:

        2023-05-01T00:05:01Z
        2023-05-01T01:20:22Z
        2023-05-01T03:35:31Z

    therefore your output must match those three lines byte-for-byte (newline-separated, no carriage returns, no trailing space).  

    A single pipeline or one-liner is sufficient; feel free to use any standard POSIX text-processing utility (grep, awk, cut, sed, etc.).
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing log file and its precise contents -->
    <file path="/home/user/logs/service_status.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-01T00:00:01Z UP 48ms
2023-05-01T00:05:01Z DOWN
2023-05-01T00:10:01Z UP 45ms
2023-05-01T01:15:22Z UP 46ms
2023-05-01T01:20:22Z DOWN
2023-05-01T02:30:31Z UP 47ms
2023-05-01T03:30:31Z UP 44ms
2023-05-01T03:35:31Z DOWN
2023-05-01T04:00:00Z UP 41ms
2023-05-01T04:30:00Z UP 42ms
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected STDOUT (oracle) -->
    <expected_stdout><![CDATA[
2023-05-01T00:05:01Z
2023-05-01T01:20:22Z
2023-05-01T03:35:31Z
]]></expected_stdout>
name: task_000000_37a884bd
